title: Code Review Findings
description: Output schema for the inc:review single-agent pass. The findings.json artifact is a JSON array of finding objects.

file.description: Relative file path from repository root

line.description: Primary line number of the issue

autofix_class.description: Reviewer's conservative recommendation for how this issue should be handled

title.description: Short, specific issue title. 10 words or fewer.

why_it_matters.description: Impact and failure mode -- not 'what is wrong' but 'what breaks'

suggested_fix.description: Concrete minimal fix. Omit or null if no good fix is obvious -- a bad suggestion is worse than none.

severity_definitions.P0: Critical breakage, exploitable vulnerability, data loss/corruption. Must fix before merge.
severity_definitions.P1: High-impact defect likely hit in normal usage, breaking contract. Should fix.
severity_definitions.P2: Moderate issue with meaningful downside (edge case, perf regression, maintainability trap). Fix if straightforward.
severity_definitions.P3: Low-impact, narrow scope, minor improvement. User's discretion.

autofix_classes.auto_apply: Local, deterministic code or test fix safe to apply automatically -- correctness, error handling, security, performance, mechanical code quality, with no question about the author's intent. Examples: extract duplicated helper, add missing nil check, fix off-by-one, add missing test, remove dead code. Do not default to fyi when a concrete safe fix exists.
autofix_classes.ask_user: A concrete issue the user should review because it changes behavior, permissions, contracts, product decisions, or otherwise touches the author's deliberate intent -- OR it is actionable work that needs a design decision or cross-cutting change. Presented to the user; never auto-applied. Examples: add auth to an unprotected endpoint, change an API response shape, redesign a data model, choose between architectural approaches.
autofix_classes.fyi: Informational or operational item surfaced in the report only -- no action required. Examples: design asymmetry the PR improves but does not fully resolve, residual risk notes, deployment considerations, acknowledged tradeoffs.
